metadata.name: ww-input-file
metadata.description: A file upload component with drag-and-drop support, file type filtering, file size validation, and a built-in file list. The only valid element for file uploads in WeWeb.
features: File type filtering (image, video, audio, pdf, etc.)
File size validation (min/max per file, max total for multi)
Built-in file list display with remove buttons
Exposes a `value` variable (array of File objects)

properties.name: type
properties.description: Upload mode: 'single' for one file, 'multi' for multiple files.

properties.name: customExtensions
properties.description: Comma-separated list of allowed file extensions when extensions is 'custom', e.g. '.jpg, .png'. Only used when extensions is 'custom'.

properties.name: maxTotalFileSize
properties.description: Maximum total size for all files in MB. Only relevant when type is 'multi'.

properties.name: maxFiles
properties.description: Maximum number of files allowed. Only relevant when type is 'multi'.

properties.name: labelMessage
properties.description: Label text shown in the upload zone.
properties.defaultValue: Drop files here or click to upload

events.name: change
events.description: Triggered when files are added or removed.

events.name: error
events.description: Triggered when file validation fails (wrong type, size exceeded, etc.).

variables.name: value
variables.description: Array of selected File objects. Each file has: name (string), size (number in bytes), type (MIME string), extension (string). Access path in workflow bindings: variables['{elementUid}-value'].

actions.label: Clear files
actions.description: Clears all files from the component.

specifications: This is the ONLY valid element for file uploads. Never use ww-input-basic with type 'file'.
Add a separate ww-button to trigger the upload workflow. The file upload does NOT use form submit.

When calling an execute-backend-workflow action that expects a file parameter, bind the file parameter as a plain string '{elementUid}-value' — NOT a formula object. Example: {"image": "a1b2c3d4-value"}.
After a successful upload, re-fetch the relevant table view to display the newly uploaded file.
Do NOT enable exposeBase64 or exposeBinary for backend upload workflows — the backend receives the raw File object via the {elementUid}-value binding.

examples.description: Standalone file upload element for image uploads, paired with an upload button.
